message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:p>
                  Since the first confirmed case of SARS-CoV-2 coronavirus (COVID-19) in the 2
                  <jats:italic>
                    <jats:sup>nd</jats:sup>
                  </jats:italic>
                  day of March, Saudi Arabia has not report a quite rapid COVD-19 spread compared to America and many European countries. Possible causes include the spread of asymptomatic cases. To characterize the transmission of COVID-19 in Saudi Arabia, this paper applies a susceptible, exposed, symptomatic, asymptomatic, hospitalized, and recovered dynamical model, along with the official COVID-19 reported data by the Ministry of Health in Saudi Arabia. The basic reproduction number
                  <jats:italic>R</jats:italic>
                  <jats:sub>0</jats:sub>
                  is estimated to range from 2.87 to 4.9.
                </jats:p>
title: A mathematical model to investigate the transmission of COVID-19 in the Kingdom of Saudi Arabia
